displayTitle: A Procedure Is a Skeleton, Not an Essay
kicker: PT LEVEL II // WRITING THE PT PROCEDURE

beats.narration: A penetrant procedure is not an essay. It is a document with a fixed skeleton, approved by a certified Level Three before it reaches the floor.

payload.title: A FIXED SKELETON
payload.sub: Write the six sections; a stranger runs your inspection.

beats.narration: Six sections, each answering one question. Foreword: does it apply? Product: what hardware? Conditions: with what? Detailed instructions: exactly how?
The foreword settles conflicts first: purchase order, then customer spec, then this procedure. One rule never bends — be more conservative, never less.

chips.s: Outside it, the procedure does not apply

chips.t: Reference docs by number and revision
Order of precedence: PO, spec, procedure

beats.narration: Detailed instructions carry an action, a numeric window, a clock, and a check, so two inspectors get the same result. Reporting proves it later.

payload.heading: What the report must capture
payload.note: ACTION · WINDOW · CLOCK · CHECK → PROCEDURE + REVISION + SIGNATURE

beats.narration: So write every section completely. A qualified stranger picks up the part, runs your inspection, and reaches your result — zero questions asked.

payload.sub: Write it complete — a stranger gets your result.
